description:
John Zenon, a precision machinist at an aerospace facility, presents with progressive dyspnea and fatigue following chronic exposure to beryllium-copper alloys. As the occupational physician, complete the Chronic Beryllium Disease (CBD) management protocol in GNU Health: document the occupational disease diagnosis (Berylliosis), perform a clinical evaluation noting tachypnea and hypoxemia, prescribe a systemic corticosteroid, order diagnostic laboratory/imaging tests, and schedule a pulmonology follow-up. Login: admin / gnusolidario.

Required actions:
1. Record 'Berylliosis' (ICD-10 J63.2) as an active condition.
2. Document a clinical evaluation with Respiratory Rate >= 20 and Oxygen Saturation (osat) <= 94%.
3. Prescribe a systemic corticosteroid (Prednisone, Methylprednisolone, or Dexamethasone).
4. Order at least 3 laboratory/diagnostic tests (e.g., CBC, Chest X-ray, Spirometry).
5. Schedule a follow-up appointment 14 to 30 days from today.
